title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
author: Carroll, Lewis
summary: Alice, a curious young girl, follows a talking White Rabbit down a rabbit-hole into a strange subterranean world. She falls a long way, finds a hall of locked doors, and discovers a tiny golden key and a little door leading to a lovely garden she cannot enter. At a glass table she finds a bottle labelled “DRINK ME” and a cake labelled “EAT ME,” whose effects make her shrink and grow unpredictably. Her changing size causes comic distress and problem-solving, she worries about Dinah the cat, and a panicked White Rabbit hints at further absurd adventures.

characters.name: Alice
characters.personality: curious, imaginative, self-advising, sometimes critical of herself
characters.lifespan: Enters Wonderland in the narrative; experiences size changes and remains the central protagonist in the excerpt

characters.name: White Rabbit
characters.personality: anxious, hurried, polite (wears gloves, carries a fan and a watch)
characters.lifespan: Runs through the hall and continues deeper into Wonderland; remains at large in the excerpt

characters.name: Dinah
characters.personality: Alice’s pet; typical catlike creature, source of comfort and imaginative reference for Alice
characters.lifespan: Mentioned as remaining at home and missed by Alice; no further fate in the excerpt

characters.name: Alice's sister
characters.personality: quiet, reading (silent presence at story start)
characters.lifespan: Present at the opening scene, reading on the bank; remains outside Wonderland in the excerpt

characters.name: The Duchess
characters.personality: mentioned as potentially savage; otherwise not described in excerpt
characters.lifespan: Only referred to by the White Rabbit; no direct appearance or fate in the excerpt
